content.problemStatement:
You are a stock trader looking to maximize your profits. You are given the daily prices of a stock over NNN days and a maximum number of transactions KKK you can perform. Each transaction consists of buying a stock on one day and selling it on a later day. You cannot engage in multiple transactions simultaneously - you must sell your current stock before buying another.

Your task is to determine the maximum profit you can achieve while performing at most KKK transactions.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK - number of days and maximum allowed transactions
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,...,PNP_1, P_2, ..., P_NP1​,P2​,...,PN​ - where PiP_iPi​ represents the stock price on day iii




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer - the maximum profit achievable

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

The best strategy is to make 1 transaction:

Buy at $2 (day 1)
Sell at $4 (day 2)
Profit = 4−4 - 4−2 = $2



Test Case 2:


We can make 2 transactions for maximum profit:


Transaction 1:

Buy at $2 (day 2)
Sell at $6 (day 3)
Profit = 6−6 - 6−2 = $4



Transaction 2:

Buy at $0 (day 5)
Sell at $3 (day 6)
Profit = 3−3 - 3−0 = $3
Total profit = 4+4 + 4+3 = $7
